topic.name: Tax Credits and Deductions
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at amending the Internal Revenue Code to provide tax credits and deductions for education, energy investments, and other specific purposes.

Document 1: allow a tax credit for contributions to a qualified tuition program
allow an exclusion from the gross income of an employee for employer contributions
Document 2: make permanent the reduction in the marriage penalty applicable to the earned income tax credit
increase such tax credit for families with three or more qualifying children
Document 3: allow through 2016 a 30% energy tax credit for investment in geothermal energy property
Document 4: increase the allowable amount of the railroad track maintenance tax credit
Document 5: allow a 30% energy tax credit for qualified microturbine property
Document 6: allow state and local real property taxes paid on a principal residence as a deduction from gross income
Document 7: allow an estate and gift tax exclusion for real property used as a farm for farming purposes
Document 8: make qualified biomass fuel property expenditures eligible for such credit
Document 9: make permanent the tax credit for increasing research activities
allow an increased 25% tax credit rate for research expenses incurred in the United States
Document 10: allow a business tax credit for the production, sale, or use of biogas

topic.name: Medicare Program Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes and enhancements to the Medicare program aimed at improving healthcare services and coverage for beneficiaries.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide coverage of diabetes prevention program services
Expresses the sense of the Senate that the National Diabetes Prevention Program presents an opportunity for states to reduce the incidence of diabetes
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to establish a demonstration project to evaluate new approaches to coding and payment under the Medicare program
Amends SSA title XVIII with respect to correction of erroneous determinations and other changes in fee schedule
Document 3: This bill declares that its purpose is to ensure beneficiary access to advance wound care products while in a skilled nursing facility
The Secretary of Health and Human Services is required to provide for the submission and processing of claims for advanced surgical dressings for payment under Medicare part B
Document 4: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide an additional 5% increase in Medicare payments for ground ambulance services
Document 5: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to require the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services to offer one or more Medicare-operated prescription drug plan options
CMS must negotiate prices with drug manufacturers
Document 6: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to extend the period reasonable cost reimbursement contracts under Medicare can remain in the market
Document 7: Amends part D (Voluntary Prescription Drug Benefit Program) of title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to provide for establishment of one or more Medicare operated prescription drug plan options
Document 8: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the 190-day lifetime limit on inpatient psychiatric hospital services
Document 9: This bill amends the Public Health Service Act to expand the scope and responsibilities of the United States Preventive Services Task Force
This bill amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to eliminate the authority of HHS to make no payment for a preventive service that has not been graded by the Task Force
Document 10: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to extend coverage to lymphedema diagnosis and treatment services

topic.name: Higher Education Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving and regulating higher education and related educational programs in the United States.

Document 1: Online Education Verification Act - Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
Document 2: Promoting Readiness through Early Pell Act of 2016 or the PREP Act of 2016
Document 3: Patsy T. Mink Fellowship Act of 2007 - Amends the Higher Education Act of 1965 to establish the Patsy T. Mink Fellowship program
Document 9: Maximizing Opportunities and Retaining Experienced Teachers Act or the MORE Act - Amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965

topic.name: U.S. Tariff and Trade Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments and acts related to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule and trade regulations in the United States.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of State to suspend all U.S. foreign assistance to Mexico, Honduras, Guatemala, and El Salvador
mitigate unlawful U.S.-Mexico border crossings
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to provide a duty on certain rayon staple fibers.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on staple fibers of viscose rayon
Document 4: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to amend the article description of certain giardiniera
Document 5: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to increase from $200 to $800 for FY2013
Document 6: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary reduction of duty on certain undyed acrylic staple fibers.
Document 7: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on 2-Chloro benzyl chloride.
Document 8: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to specifically provide for duty-free treatment of certain tuna and textile and apparel.
Document 9: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on certain electric dispensing blenders.
Document 10: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on small particle calcium chloride phosphor.

topic.name: Veterans Affairs Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving healthcare, benefits, and employment opportunities for veterans and their families.

Document 1: authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to provide an effective date of an award of disability compensation
in the case of a veteran who submits a fully-developed claim
Document 2: directs the Secretaries of Defense and Veterans Affairs to establish a process to provide payments for treatments of traumatic brain injury (TBI) and post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD)
ensure that the database ensures patient confidentiality
Document 3: requires the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to ensure that veterans eligible for hospital care and medical services through the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) have access to at least one full-service VA hospital
directs the Secretary to report to Congress on compliance with such requirement
Document 4: reduces from three years to one year the period during which a disabled veterans' outreach program specialist or a local veterans' employment representative must complete the training program
Document 5: authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to provide medical care to a family member of a disabled veteran
prohibits the caregiver from being subject to deductibles, premiums, copayments, or other fees for such care
Document 6: authorizes the Secretary of Veterans Affairs (VA) to reimburse any full-time board-certified health professional for continuing professional education expenses
prohibits reimbursement for the same expenses incurred for continuing professional education provided by a VA medical center
Document 7: requires the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to furnish, within 14 days after receiving a request, any record in its custody pertaining to a claim
the VA is unable to furnish a record by such time, it shall inform the requester within 10 days
Document 8: instructs the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) to consider all qualified veterans before any non-veterans for employment
requires the Director of the VA to submit annual reports to Congress with respect to the hiring of veterans
Document 9: eliminates the requirement limiting reimbursement for emergency treatment in a non-Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) facility to veterans who are active participants in the VA's health care system
treats the VA as a participating provider for purposes of allowing the VA to recover the costs
Document 10: authorizes the city of Aurora, Colorado, to convey by donation to the United States nonfederal lands for construction of a veterans' medical facility

topic.name: Oil and Gas Regulation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative amendments and acts related to the regulation, environmental impact, and workforce training for oil and gas infrastructure in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Alaska Natural Gas Pipeline Act to add to the purpose of any grants by the Secretary of Labor to support a program to train workers in the construction, operation, maintenance, and performance of all related environmental processes involving existing and future oil and gas infrastructure in Alaska.
Increases from 15% to 50% the amount of federal funds that may be used for such training facility.
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act to repeal prohibitions against aggregating emissions from any oil or gas exploration or production well and emissions from any pipeline compressor or pump station.
Requires the Administrator to issue a final rule adding hydrogen sulfide to the list of hazardous air pollutants.
Document 3: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to direct the Secretary of Energy to establish a fee for any nonproducing oil or gas leases on outer Continental Shelf land.
Establishes a fee for any nonproducing oil or gas leases on outer Continental Shelf land in the Gulf of Mexico.
Document 5: Instructs the Secretary of the Interior to issue a new oil and gas leasing program under the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act for a five-year period.
Prescribes requirements for development of proposed new leasing programs.
Document 9: Amends the Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act to revise the policy concerning resource development on the outer Continental Shelf to include emphasis on protection, maintenance, and restoration of the marine and coastal environments.

Authorizes the leasing, exploration, and development of oil and gas resources of the OCS only if the Secretary of the Interior has a thorough understanding of the marine coastal environments affected by the activity.

topic.name: Immigration Policy and Reform
topic.description: The documents discuss various aspects of immigration policy, including provisions for nonimmigrant visas, pathways to legal status for certain groups, and enforcement measures related to immigration.

Document 1: authorizes the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to cancel the removal of, and adjust to conditional nonimmigrant for an initial five-year period the status of, an alien who was younger than 16 years old when he or she initially entered the United States
has not participated in the persecution of any person on account of race, religion, nationality, membership in a particular social group, or political opinion
Document 2: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include within the H-2A nonimmigrant visa category an alien coming temporarily to the United States to work as a sheepherder, goat herder, or dairy worker
provides for a three-year initial period of admission with additional three-year extensions
Document 3: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require the detention of an alien who was not inspected and admitted into the United States
U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement shall make reasonable efforts to obtain information about the identity of any victims of the crimes for which such alien was charged or convicted
Document 4: requires state plans for foster care and adoption assistance to have procedures to assist alien children in the child welfare system achieve special immigrant juvenile status
requires a permanency hearing to determine whether a petition for special immigrant juvenile status or lawful permanent resident status has been filed
Document 5: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to direct the Attorney General (DOJ) to take into custody an alien who is unlawfully in the United States

requires a state or locality, in order to qualify for state criminal alien assistance program (SCAAP) funding, to participate by January 1, 2014, in either or both of the secure communities initiative
Document 6: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to consider a person born in the United States 'subject to the jurisdiction' of the United States for citizenship at birth purposes
if the person is born in the United States of parents, one of whom is a U.S. citizen or national
Document 7: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to establish an annual cap on H-1B nonimmigrant visas
directs the Secretary of Homeland Security (DHS) to authorize the accompanying spouse of an H-1B alien to work in the United States
Document 8: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to make up to 55,000 visas available in FY2014 and subsequent fiscal years to qualified immigrants who have a doctorate degree in a field of science, technology, engineering, or mathematics (STEM degree)
defines 'United States doctoral institution of higher education'
Document 9: declares that the Kurdistan Democratic Party and the Patriotic Union of Kurdistan shall not be considered terrorist organizations under the Immigration and Nationality Act
authorizes the Secretary of State to suspend this declaration for either or both groups
Document 10: revises the H-2A nonimmigrant agricultural worker visa program
includes provisions regarding agricultural labor and services, application requirements, wages, period of authorized nonimmigrant status

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons
topic.description: Legislation focused on the assessment and reporting of foreign countries' programs related to electromagnetic pulse weapons.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Federal Land Management and Conservation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to the management, conveyance, and conservation of federal lands, particularly concerning timber, wilderness areas, and community needs.

Document 1: Amends the Healthy Forests Restoration Act of 2003 to direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) and the Secretary of the Interior...
Directs the Secretary to initiate salvage activities on federal land affected by a catastrophic event...
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey to the local communities in South Dakota that are currently managing certain community cemeteries...
Requires the conveyed lands to continue to be used in the same manner and for the same purposes as they were used immediately before their conveyance.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior... to modify the contract to add three years to such contract's expiration date.
Prohibits anything in this Act from affecting any claim by the United States against any timber purchaser...
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey to the city of Fruit Heights, Utah, all interest of the United States in approximately 100 acres of identified National Forest System land.
Requires the city to use such land only for public purposes.
Document 5: This bill releases the following areas in Colorado from further review for designation as wilderness...
The bill states that in areas in which the Colorado River is used as a reference for defining the boundary of McInnis Canyons National Conservation Area...
Document 6: Designates certain Bureau of Land Management (BLM) land in Oregon as wilderness...
Permits established livestock grazing in the Wilderness to continue.
Document 7: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, as the Rocky Mountain National Park Wilderness...
Provides for the inclusion of specified potential wilderness land within the Park in such Wilderness.
Document 8: Requires the Secretary of Agriculture, acting through the Chief of the Forest Service... to convey specified parcels of federal land to certain counties and cities in central Idaho.
Designates specified parcels of federal land in central Idaho as wilderness areas to be managed as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Document 9: This bill amends the Washington Park Wilderness Act of 1988 to redesignate the Olympic Wilderness as the 'Daniel J. Evans Wilderness.'
Document 10: Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to add specified lands in Colorado managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM)...
Designates specified lands in Colorado managed by the BLM or the U.S. Forest Service as wilderness and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.

topic.name: Child Trafficking and Welfare
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving the identification, support, and protection of children affected by trafficking and abuse.

Document 1: require a state plan for its child protective services system to include a certified assurance that the state has in effect and is enforcing a law requiring identification and assessment of all reports involving children known or suspected to be victims of sex trafficking
training child protective services workers about identifying, assessing, and providing comprehensive services for children who are sex trafficking victims.
Document 3: develop guidelines for use by state child welfare agencies in training appropriate child welfare employees and court employees in identifying, documenting, educating, and counseling children at risk of becoming victims of trafficking
recommendations on how state child welfare agencies may prevent children from becoming victims of trafficking.
Document 5: authorize the Attorney General to make grants to state and local governments, Indian tribal governments, courts and other specified providers for supervised visitation and safe visitation exchange of children and youth in situations involving domestic violence, dating violence, child sexual abuse
ensure that by January 1, 2016, a court-appointed special advocate shall be available to every victim of child abuse or neglect in the United States that needs one.
Document 10: require the state plan under a grant from the Secretary of Health and Human Services for child abuse and neglect prevention and treatment programs to contain an assurance in the form of a certification by the state governor

prescribing a criminal penalty against an individual age 18 or older who fails to report to a state or local law enforcement official or child protective services that the individual has witnessed another individual in the state engaging in sexual abuse of a child.

topic.name: Mental Health and Autism Services
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving mental health services and support for individuals with autism spectrum disorders and other mental health conditions.

Document 1: Autism Treatment Acceleration Act of 2009
improve the effectiveness and efficiency in providing comprehensive care to individuals diagnosed with autism spectrum disorders
Document 2: Mental Health Awareness and Improvement Act of 2015
enhancement of services for students with mental health or substance use disorders at institutions of higher education
Document 3: Mental Health on Campus Improvement Act
improve mental and behavioral health services and outreach on college and university campuses
Document 4: Classified Veterans Access to Care Act
access VA mental health care in a manner that fully accommodates the veteran's obligation
Document 5: Helping HANDS for Autism Act
create a more efficient, effective, coordinated use of the health, housing, education, and social service systems for individuals with an autism spectrum disorder
Document 6: Autism Spectrum Disorders Services Act of 2011
expand activities to include coordinating efforts of all federal agencies
Document 7: Behavioral Health Information Technology Act of 2012
include as a health care provider behavioral and mental health professionals
Document 8: Community Mental Health Services Improvement Act
provide coordinated and integrated services to individuals with mental illnesses
Document 9: Mental Health on Campus Improvement Act
coordinate and evaluating a targeted public education campaign that is designed to focus on mental and behavioral health on college campuses
Document 10: Community Assistance Act for Persons with Mental Illness
provide housing counseling and financial counseling for individuals before their release from institutions for individuals with mental illness

topic.name: Drug Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures related to the regulation, safety, and accessibility of pharmaceuticals and controlled substances in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to deem any drug or device to be adulterated if it contains mercury intended for use in a dental filling.
Deems a device that contains mercury intended for use in a dental filling to be misbranded.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require that priority review vouchers are transferable.
Prohibits such vouchers from being redeemed to affect the review of a pending human drug application.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Secretary of Health and Human Services to prohibit the license holder of a Food and Drug Administration (FDA)-approved drug from restricting availability of the drug for testing.
Establishes a process for a product developer to obtain reasonable quantities of an approved drug when those quantities are not available commercially.
Document 4: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to promulgate regulations permitting individuals to import a prescription drug purchased from an approved foreign pharmacy.
Establishes a certification process for approving pharmacies in certain foreign countries.
Document 5: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to modify the drug quantity thresholds that trigger a mandatory minimum prison term for fentanyl.
Reduces the fentanyl quantity and analogue quantity that trigger mandatory minimum prison terms.
Document 6: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to establish a new federal registration process for marijuana research.
Establishes a process for registering persons to manufacture or distribute marijuana for research or commercial production.
Document 7: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to establish requirements to facilitate the tracing of drug products through the pharmaceutical supply distribution chain.
Requires a manufacturer, wholesale distributor, dispenser, repackager to ensure that each of its trading partners is authorized.
Document 8: Requires the Department of Health and Human Services to issue draft guidance on the dissemination of scientific and medical information that is not included in the approved labeling of drugs and medical devices.
Document 9: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require HHS to extend by six months the exclusivity period for a drug approved for a new indication that is a rare disease.
Limits a product to one extension under this Act.
Document 10: Amends the Public Health Service Act to require the CDC to study strategies for preventing and treating the use of synthetic recreational drugs.
The CDC must develop and implement a national outreach campaign to educate about preventing and treating the use of substances similar to marijuana.

topic.name: Water Resource Management
topic.description: The documents focus on legislation and projects aimed at the restoration, management, and enhancement of water resources and ecosystems across various regions in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior...
to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility and recycled water system.
Document 2: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior...
to enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem.
Document 3: Directs the Chief of Engineers, in coordination with Los Angeles County, California, to prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration...
for construction and restoration of the San Gabriel River Watershed.
Document 4: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality...
in accordance with the recommendations of the report of the Chief of Engineers.
Document 5: Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement...
Sets forth provisions regarding the waiver, release, and retention of specified water rights claims.
Document 6: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control...
in accordance with the recommendations of the report of the Chief of Engineers.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, in coordination with Los Angeles County, California, to prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration...
to construct demonstration projects to provide information to develop the study.
Document 8: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior...
to enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem.

topic.name: Mortgage Reform and Consumer Protection
topic.description: Legislation aimed at reforming mortgage practices and protecting consumers in the housing market.

Document 1: Requires GSEs to establish a program to provide for the refinancing of eligible mortgages.
Defines eligible mortgage as an existing first mortgage on a 1- to 4-family owner-occupied dwelling.
Document 2: Prohibits mortgagees from imposing interest charges due to the loss of any repayment time period.
Amends the National Housing Act to protect borrowers from excessive interest payments.
Document 3: Prohibits commencement of a foreclosure action unless specified requirements are met regarding borrower notification and assistance.
Makes a violation of this Act a bar to a foreclosure action.
Document 4: Prohibits actions that limit the scope of GSEs in multifamily housing mortgages without substantial evidence.
Ensures the financial safety and soundness of GSEs.
Document 5: Amends the Truth in Lending Act regarding prohibitions against steering incentives in mortgage origination.
Declares that certain compensation structures for mortgage originators are permissible.
Document 6: Establishes a commission to study the mortgage lending crisis and recommend changes to prevent future crises.
Assesses the impact of predatory lending practices and subprime abuses.
Document 7: Prohibits mortgagees from initiating foreclosure without determining eligibility for loan modification.
Requires mortgagees to provide documentation regarding loan modification options.
Document 8: Amends bankruptcy code to assist debtors facing foreclosure by modifying claim holders' rights.
Eliminates pre-petition credit counseling requirement for certain debtors.
Document 9: Amends the Housing and Community Development Act regarding mortgage-backed securities and options by Freddie Mac.
Directs Freddie Mac to ensure taxpayer benefits from excess proceeds.
Document 10: Establishes the Office of the Homeowner Advocate to assist homeowners in resolving issues with mortgage modifications.
Prohibits modifications unless the servicer determines the mortgagor cannot afford payments.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster encompasses various legislative acts aimed at the conservation, restoration, and sustainable management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Coral Reef Conservation Amendments Act of 2007
direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Document 2: Coral Reef Conservation Amendments Act of 2009
Directs the Secretary to give priority to community-based local action strategies when awarding certain grants for conservation projects.
Document 3: Coral Reef Sustainability Through Innovation Act of 2016
authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force...prize competitions that promote coral reef research and conservation.
Document 4: Coral Reef Conservation Act Reauthorization and Enhancement Amendments of 2016
NOAA and the Department of the Interior are authorized to undertake emergency actions to minimize injury to a coral reef.
Document 5: Coral Reef Conservation Act Reauthorization and Enhancement Amendments of 2009
Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds.
Document 6: Coral Reef Conservation Amendments Act of 2007
extends the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds.
Document 7: Coral Reef Conservation Amendments Act of 2011
Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Document 8: Conserving Our Reefs and Livelihoods Act of 2016
expand the coral reef conservation program by awarding remaining program grant funds.
Document 9: Coral Reef Ecosystem Conservation Amendments Act of 2007
authorizes the Secretary to conduct activities...to conserve coral reef ecosystems.
Document 10: Coral Reef Conservation Act Reauthorization and Enhancement Amendments of 2011
authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function.

topic.name: Agricultural Policy Legislation
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at improving agricultural practices, supporting farmers, and enhancing food security in the United States.

Document 1: Dairy Margin Insurance Location Calculation Act of 2016
amends the Agricultural Act of 2014
Document 2: Beginning Farmer and Rancher Opportunity Act of 2013
assistance to disadvantaged or beginning farmers or ranchers
Document 3: Shellfish Equity Act of 2011
includes shellfish as an eligible crop
Document 4: suspends specified agricultural price support authorities
with regard to covered commodities, peanuts, sugar, and milk
Document 5: Biodiesel Education and Expansion Act of 2007
increases funding for the biodiesel fuel education program
Document 6: Extend Not Cut SNAP Benefits Act
extends benefit calculation for the supplemental nutrition assistance program
Document 7: Healthy, Hunger-Free Kids Act of 2010
amends the National School Lunch Act to improve meal access
Document 8: Rural Education Achievement Program Reauthorization Act of 2007
gives rural local educational agencies federal formula grants
Document 9: Wheat Counter-Cyclical Payments Enhancement Act
authorizes the Secretary of Agriculture to consider market price variations
Document 10: Aggregate Risk and Revenue Management Act of 2011
directs USDA to manage risk and revenue payments for producers

topic.name: Low-Carbon Transportation Initiatives
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts and programs aimed at promoting low-carbon and renewable energy solutions in the transportation sector.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel standard for FY2009-FY2012.
Requires the average lifecycle GHG emissions of the aggregate quantity for 2012 to be at least 3% below the 2007 average.
Document 2: Establishes within the Department of Energy (DOE) a national plug-in electric drive vehicle deployment program.
Directs the Secretary of Energy to provide technical assistance to state, local, and tribal governments with the deployment of plug-in electric drive vehicles.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to require the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) to establish and maintain a system under which credits are certified for compliance with the renewable fuel program.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2014 the date by which specified alternative or renewable energy facilities must be placed in service to qualify for the electricity production tax credit.
Document 5: Authorizes the Secretary of Energy to make grants to encourage cooperation between the United States and China in joint ventures involving energy and climate change policy education programs.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Energy to establish a program to provide loan guarantees for the construction of facilities for the manufacture of advanced vehicle batteries.
Provides grants to state governments to carry out projects to encourage the use of plug-in electric drive vehicles.

topic.name: Data Privacy and Security Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at protecting personally identifiable information and ensuring data security for individuals and organizations.

Document 1: the General Services Administration to procure an antivirus product suitable for federal agencies
provide to any individual directly affected a one-year license of such product.
Document 2: Requires any entity that collects, sells, discloses for consideration, or uses personally identifiable information
implement an information security policy that is applicable to the information security practices and treatment of personally identifiable information.
Document 3: develop, implement, and maintain reasonable security and notification procedures and practices
notify any individual whose sensitive personal information was compromised.
Document 4: expand requirements for reports to Congress on acquisition or disclosure of unsecured protected health information
require the Attorney General to report annually to Congress.
Document 5: Requires any agency or business entity with sensitive personally identifiable information to notify without unreasonable delay
Requires the notification to individuals whose sensitive personally identifiable information has been acquired.
Document 6: provides the public free electronic access to all congressionally mandated reports
must redact from such a report any information that may not be publicly released under the Freedom of Information Act.
Document 7: establish policies, procedures, and standards for agencies to follow in the event of a breach of data security
timely notification to individuals whose sensitive personal information could be compromised.
Document 8: Defines 'sensitive personally identifiable information' to include specified combinations of data elements
Enhancing Punishment for Identity Theft and Other Violations of Data Privacy and Security.
Document 9: provide criminal penalties for intentional failures to provide required notices of a security breach
Defines 'security breach' as a compromise of the security, confidentiality, or integrity of computerized data.
Document 10: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure
Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as return, return information, and taxpayer return information.

topic.name: Anti-Money Laundering and Fraud Prevention
topic.description: Legislation aimed at strengthening measures against money laundering, tax fraud, and identity theft in financial systems.

Document 1: Amends federal law governing anti-money laundering programs
Sets forth civil and criminal penalties for violations of this Act
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of the Treasury to impose restrictions on foreign jurisdictions
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to increase penalties for promoting abusive tax shelters
Document 4: Amends the federal criminal code to redefine 'specified unlawful activity' for purposes of money laundering provisions
Increases the penalty for bulk cash smuggling in or out of the United States
Document 5: Establish requirements and authorities for the IRS related to preventing identity theft and tax refund fraud
Increases civil and criminal penalties associated with identify theft
Document 8: Requires the Department of the Treasury and the IRS to take actions to prevent tax-related identity theft
Establishes a centralized point of contact for taxpayers affected by identity theft
Document 9: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to require U.S. entities to obtain an employer identification number
Establishes civil and criminal penalties for failure to comply with EIN requirements

topic.name: Community Banking Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts and amendments aimed at revising regulatory requirements and oversight for community banks and depository institutions in the United States.

Document 1: Revises regulatory requirements for community banks
Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to publish proposed revisions
Document 2: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to require the risk-based assessment system
Directs the Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) to impose a systemic risk assessment
Document 4: Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to publish proposed revisions to the Small Bank Holding Company Policy Statement
Amends the Truth in Lending Act to require the Board to exempt from certain escrow or impound requirements
Document 9: Amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to authorize the Financial Stability Oversight Council to determine that a bank holding company shall be subject to enhanced supervision
Requires that final determination to be based upon specified factors using an indicator-based measurement approach

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at the conservation and management of endangered and threatened species, as well as their habitats.

Document 1: American Burying Beetle Relief Act of 2014 - Removes the United States Fish and Wildlife Service's listing of the American burying beetle as an endangered species.
Document 2: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to direct the Secretary of the Interior...to count all of the species without regard to whether it is found on state, private, or tribal lands.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to issue a permit for the importation of polar bear parts taken in a sport hunt in Canada.
Document 4: Amends the Wetlands Loan Act to increase and extend funding for conservation of migratory waterfowl and habitat essential to the preservation of such waterfowl.
Document 5: This bill amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to revise standards for determining what constitutes baiting for purposes of the prohibition on taking migratory game birds.
